task.objective: Enhance the `get_flaky_tests` MCP tool with additional filtering options including time window (since date), branch filter, and include_resolved flag for historical analysis of flaky tests.

context.project_type: E2E Test Dashboard - MCP Server Tools
context.background:
The `get_flaky_tests` tool returns tests sorted by flakiness rate, but it only supports basic filtering: - limit: Max results (default 10) - min_runs: Minimum runs to be considered flaky (default 5)
Missing filters that would improve usefulness: - since: Only consider flaky runs since a specific date - branch: Filter to see flakiness on specific branches (e.g., main vs feature) - include_resolved: Show tests that were flaky but are now stable
These additions enable: - Tracking flakiness trends over time ("Did flakiness improve this week?") - Branch-specific analysis ("Is this test flaky on main or just feature branches?") - Historical review ("What tests were flaky that we fixed?")
context.audience: - AI assistants triaging flaky tests - Developers debugging intermittent failures - QA teams measuring flakiness reduction
context.impact: MEDIUM - Significantly improves flaky test analysis workflows. Critical for teams actively working on reducing test flakiness.

issue.fix: Add since parameter to filter by recency
Add branch parameter (requires joining with test_executions)
Add include_resolved to show tests that became stable

issue.note: Time-based filtering requires different approach - either new table or join with test_results

functional.requirement: Add since parameter to filter by last_flaky_at date
Add branch parameter for branch-specific flakiness
Return last_branch field showing where test was last flaky

non_functional.requirement: Efficient query - avoid N+1 or full table scans
Backwards compatible - existing calls should work

solution.approach: 1. Add options object to getFlakiestTests function 2. Add since filter using last_flaky_at column 3. Add branch filter via subquery or join 4. Add include_resolved flag to include flaky_runs = 0 5. Update tool definition and handler
solution.interface: interface GetFlakiestTestsOptions { limit?: number minRuns?: number since?: string // ISO date - only tests flaky since this date branch?: string // Filter to specific branch includeResolved?: boolean // Include tests no longer flaky (default: false) }

steps.step: 1. Start dev server: npm run dev
2. Call get_flaky_tests with no parameters - verify backwards compatibility
3. Call with since="2026-01-01" - verify time filtering
4. Call with branch="main" - verify branch filtering
5. Call with include_resolved=true - verify resolved tests appear
6. Verify last_flaky_branch field is present in response

test.script:
# Test 1: Default behavior (backwards compatible) curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/mcp/tools \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer $MCP_TOKEN" \ -d '{"tool": "get_flaky_tests", "args": {}}'
# Test 2: Time filter curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/mcp/tools \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer $MCP_TOKEN" \ -d '{"tool": "get_flaky_tests", "args": {"since": "2026-01-01"}}'
# Test 3: Branch filter curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/mcp/tools \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer $MCP_TOKEN" \ -d '{"tool": "get_flaky_tests", "args": {"branch": "main"}}'
# Test 4: Include resolved curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/mcp/tools \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer $MCP_TOKEN" \ -d '{"tool": "get_flaky_tests", "args": {"include_resolved": true}}'

implementation_order.step: Update getFlakiestTests function to accept options
Add since, branch, and includeResolved filtering logic

always.rule: Default include_resolved to false (most users want active flaky tests)
Return last_flaky_branch for context on where flakiness occurs
Maintain existing sorting: flakiness_rate DESC, flaky_runs DESC

never.rule: Never return tests below min_runs threshold
Never break backwards compatibility with existing calls
Never expose SQL errors to clients

documentation_updates.description: The MCP documentation page must be updated to reflect the new filtering parameters (since, branch, include_resolved) and the new last_flaky_branch response field.

update.current_code: { name: "get_flaky_tests", description: "Get list of flaky tests sorted by flakiness rate. A test is flaky if it passes after retries.", category: "flakiness" as const, parameters: [ { name: "limit", type: "number", default: "10", description: "Max results" }, { name: "min_runs", type: "number", default: "5", description: "Minimum runs to be considered" }, ], responseFields: [ "test_signature: string - Unique identifier", "test_name: string - Test title", "test_file: string - Spec file path", "flakiness_rate: number (0-100)", "total_runs: number", "flaky_runs: number", "last_flaky: ISO datetime", ], example: `"What are our flakiest tests?"`, },
update.updated_code: { name: "get_flaky_tests", description: "Get list of flaky tests sorted by flakiness rate. Supports filtering by time window, branch, and resolution status.", category: "flakiness" as const, parameters: [ { name: "limit", type: "number", default: "10", description: "Max results" }, { name: "min_runs", type: "number", default: "5", description: "Minimum runs to be considered" }, { name: "since", type: "string", description: "Only tests flaky since date (ISO 8601)" }, { name: "branch", type: "string", description: "Filter to tests flaky on this branch" }, { name: "include_resolved", type: "boolean", default: "false", description: "Include tests no longer flaky" }, ], responseFields: [ "test_signature: string - Unique identifier", "test_name: string - Test title", "test_file: string - Spec file path", "flakiness_rate: number (0-100)", "total_runs: number", "flaky_runs: number", "last_flaky: ISO datetime", "last_flaky_branch: string | null - Branch where last flaky", ], example: `"What tests are flaky on the main branch?"`, },

update.note: Update the /api/flaky-tests endpoint documentation to include the new since, branch, and include_resolved parameters if the API also exposes these.

files_to_modify.file: lib/mcp/tools.ts - Update tool definition and handler

output_format.complexity: Medium - Requires subquery for branch filtering
